name: Froggy Hop
desc: The bouncy 8-bit loop from the Frog vs Toad game — now with pad/lead automation, per-instrument envelopes and crash/clap accents.

name: Cinematic
desc: A ~2-minute epic in A minor — intro, theme, bridge, climax and outro with strings, horn, brass and arps, swelling into the climax via automation.

name: Techno
desc: A ~2-minute 128 BPM journey — intro, groove, build, drop, breakdown and a bigger second drop, with riser automation and backbeat claps into each drop.

name: Neon Drive
desc: An original 80s synth-pop song at 120 BPM — verse, chorus, bridge and a soaring final chorus, with a sequenced arp, lush swelling pad, synth-bass slides and a proper melodic hook.

name: Popcorn
desc: A chiptune interpretation of the classic bouncy synth riff — a punchy square-wave hook alternating between two keys a whole step apart, with a contrasting bridge.
